message:
fix(markdown): preserve cell links and styling in table rendering

The table cell rendering path was reconstructing `Span` from a string,
which dropped the `link` field unless explicitly re-attached, and
even then only re-attached for spans with an existing link. Combined
with the upstream string-based reconstruction, styling/link attributes
on rich spans were lost when rows were rendered.

Changes:
- `render_table_row` now borrows the cell's span slice directly via
  `Vec::as_slice` instead of cloning into a fresh Vec, removing the
  unnecessary allocation per cell per row.
- `apply_base_style` clones each input `Span` (preserving content,
  link, and any other span fields) and only overwrites `style` after
  merging with `base_style`. This is strictly more correct than the
  prior approach of rebuilding `Span::styled(content, style)` and
  conditionally re-applying the link, which lost any future fields.
- Adds `markdown_table_preserves_cell_links` regression test that
  parses a table with a markdown link in a cell and asserts the
  rendered span still carries the original href.
